messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "I think it's really important to be able to handle conversations about sensitive topics like healthcare and COVID vaccines. As someone who has a master's degree, even though I work as a cleaner in Chicago, I've learned that knowledge gives me confidence when discussing these things. For example, my husband is our family doctor, so he knows all the ins and outs of it, and we always consult him on important decisions about health.

When people start talking to me about vaccines or treatments online, even though I don't spend much time there—just a few times monthly—I feel like I need to be careful. My personality is quite open, but not too outgoing; the ocean test says high extraversion, so I do enjoy meeting people and sharing ideas during my free time when I'm cycling or going hiking.

I believe in being honest but respectful. If someone asks me what I think, I share our family's perspective without pushing anyone else to agree. It helps that I'm moderately agreeable—it makes conversations smoother—but sometimes the stress gets to me because of high neuroticism and moderate openness. Still, managing my own mental health is key so I don't get overwhelmed.",
  "reasoning_summary": "I start by considering how personal experiences shape opinions on sensitive topics. My master's degree provides a foundation for understanding complex issues like vaccines, even though my current job as cleaner doesn't directly relate to healthcare. Family support through my husband being involved in medicine adds credibility.

Next, I address the role of communication style based on personality traits—moderate openness allows curiosity but not deep dives into uncomfortable topics; low conscientiousness means relying more on others' expertise than systematic knowledge;
high extraversion helps with social interactions but could lead to fatigue without proper boundaries;
moderate agreeableness avoids conflict while acknowledging different viewpoints.

Finally, I incorporate the impact of media usage by balancing occasional online exposure with personal offline experiences from hobbies like cycling and cinema.",
  "tone": "Calm yet approachable",
  "citations": [],
  "safety_notes": "",
  "used_persona_fields": ["age", "gender", "occupation", "education_level", "current_marital_status", "hobbies", "ocean"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
I think it's really important to be able to handle conversations about sensitive topics like healthcare and COVID vaccines. As someone who has a master's degree, even though I work as a cleaner in Chicago, I've learned that knowledge gives me confidence when discussing these things. For example, my husband is our family doctor, so he knows all the ins and outs of it, and we always consult him on important decisions about health.

When people start talking to me about vaccines or treatments online, even though I don't spend much time there—just a few times monthly—I feel like I need to be careful. My personality is quite open, but not too outgoing; the ocean test says high extraversion, so I do enjoy meeting people and sharing ideas during my free time when I'm cycling or going hiking.

I believe in being honest but respectful. If someone asks me what I think, I share our family's perspective without pushing anyone else to agree. It helps that I'm moderately agreeable—it makes conversations smoother—but sometimes the stress gets to me because of high neuroticism and moderate openness. Still, managing my own mental health is key so I don't get overwhelmed.
